summary: auctions-aftermarket (POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/aftermarket/listings/bids) now requires a JSON body piped via --stdin instead of hardcoding an empty body; without --stdin it errors (exit 2), and an empty/invalid body fails to parse rather than being sent.
reason: Greptile P-level: body was hardcoded `map[string]any{}` and POSTed verbatim with no body input, so the bid command always sent an empty {} body (silent no-op). The endpoint places multiple bids and has no per-field body flags, so it mirrors the require-stdin array-body pattern from domains records replace.
validated_outcome: No --stdin exits 2 with '--stdin is required'; empty --stdin fails JSON parse (no {} sent); a valid `[{...}]` body via --stdin --dry-run shows the array body in the request preview.
